Name: Okta STIG - Global Session Policy
Description: DISA STIG requirements for Okta Global Session Policy configuration. These checks validate session management, cookie persistence, network zone enforcement, and DOD notice banner display.

Requirements.Name: Default Global Session Policy must have a non-default rule with Maximum Okta global session idle time of 15 minutes
Requirements.Description: The Default Global Session Policy must have an active Priority 1 rule (not the built-in Default Rule) with Maximum Okta global session idle time set to 15 minutes or less.

Attributes.SectionDescription: Checks related to Okta Global Session Policy session management settings.

Requirements.Name: Global Session Policy must limit Maximum Okta global session lifetime to 18 hours
Requirements.Description: The Global Session Policy must limit Maximum Okta global session lifetime to 18 hours (1080 minutes) to force periodic reauthentication.

Attributes.SectionDescription: Checks related to Okta Global Session Policy session management settings.

Requirements.Name: Okta global session cookies persist across browser sessions must be Disabled in the Default Global Session Policy
Requirements.Description: The Default Global Session Policy must have persistent cookies disabled in all active rules to prevent session reuse across browser restarts.

Attributes.SectionDescription: Checks related to Okta Global Session Policy session management settings.

Requirements.Name: Global Session Policy must use IF User's IP is condition mapped to a Network Zone
Requirements.Description: The Global Session Policy must use an 'IF User's IP is' condition mapped to a Network Zone for allow/deny per Access Control Policy.

Attributes.SectionDescription: Checks related to Okta Global Session Policy network zone enforcement.

Requirements.Name: Sign-in widget/brand must display the Standard Mandatory DOD Notice and Consent Banner before login
Requirements.Description: The Okta sign-in widget or brand must display the Standard Mandatory DOD Notice and Consent Banner (DTM-08-060) before login. Partially auditable via API; non-determinable items reported as MANUAL.

Attributes.SectionDescription: Checks related to Okta sign-in page branding and customization.
